candidate: SCUTE-MEMORY: 2D hexagonal-tiled KV cache shards with 6-neighbor overlap; union retrieval for robust long-context (pangolin-armor inspired).

per_result_judgments.reason: Source-domain biology only; no LLM/KV mechanism.
Shared KV cache with prefix-reuse is 1D; lacks hex tiling and 6-neighbor union.
Defensive aggregation for eviction but no 2D hexagonal tiling pattern.
Layer-wise matmul approximation; different mechanism entirely.
Compression + tiered eviction; not spatial hex overlap.
Polyhedral compilation overlapped tiles; not KV cache nor hex pattern.
Direct twin: 2D hex-tile KV cache shards with 6-neighbor union retrieval. All three thresholds exceeded.
2D spatial KV partitioning with neighbor redundancy; matches SCUTE-MEMORY core architecture (lattice may differ but functional twin).

summary: Two direct prior-art twins exist: arXiv 2604.09015 is a near-exact match (hex-tile KV shards with 6-neighbor union, sem 0.95, func 0.95) and arXiv 2511.10920 covers 2D spatial KV partitioning with neighbor overlap (sem 0.88, func 0.88). Multiple semantic and functional hits above 0.85 indicate SCUTE-MEMORY's distinctive 2D-hex-tiled overlap claim is already published. Verdict: high overlap, not novel.
